commit.message:
Replace React Native mobile app with Capacitor

Delete the entire Expo/React Native mobile app and replace it with a
Capacitor project that wraps the live Vercel web app. This eliminates
all duplicated code (parser, state management, API client, UI).

- New Capacitor project loads bball-stats-vert.vercel.app in native iOS shell
- AudioStreamPlugin.swift ports existing AVAudioEngine logic for USB mic (DJI Mic Mini)
- MyViewController registers the plugin with Capacitor bridge
- Web app detects Capacitor via isNativeAudioAvailable() and conditionally
  uses native audio (linear16 PCM) instead of MediaRecorder (webm/opus)
- Browser behavior completely unchanged
- Updated build-ios.yml workflow for Capacitor (cap sync instead of expo prebuild)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: interactive research intake with displayOptionList (#30)

* feat: interactive research intake with displayOptionList auto-continuation

Replace ask_question tool with displayOptionList for interactive research
intake. The AI presents clickable options for ambiguous queries, and after
the user confirms their selection, the chat auto-continues via
sendAutomaticallyWhen with a 400ms delay for visual feedback.

- Add tool-result continuation flow (client detects, server handles)
- Bypass prepareMessages for tool-result (no new user message to persist)
- Fix stale trigger types in BaseStreamConfig and analytics
- Use existing isToolTypePart guard instead of (p: any) casts
- Update search-mode prompts with displayOptionList intake guidelines

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate messages and related questions on tool-result continuations

Pass originalMessages to createUIMessageStream for tool-result continuations
so the SDK reuses the existing assistant message ID instead of generating a
new one. Without this, the client receives a new ID in the stream start chunk
and pushes a duplicate assistant message (with all original parts duplicated).

Also skip streamRelatedQuestions for tool-result continuations since they are
mid-research, not final answers.

Fixes: duplicate option list cards, spurious "Related" sections, and research
plan stopping early due to fragmented message history.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: remove bracket syntax from display tool examples in system prompt

The BAD/GOOD examples used [displayTable: ...] and [displayTimeline: ...]
bracket notation inside code blocks. The model mimicked this literally,
outputting display tool invocations as text instead of making actual tool
calls. Replaced with prose descriptions that separate the example text
from the tool call instruction.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* refactor: clean up tool-result flow and remove legacy question tool

- Switch tool-result continuation from full messages array to minimal
  toolResult delta (toolCallId + output) for efficiency
- Add trigger validation with typed VALID_TRIGGERS constant
- Remove legacy ask_question tool, schema, and QuestionConfirmation component
- Add ToolResultDelta type to BaseStreamConfig
- Add isInteractiveToolPart and isToolTypePart type guards
- Clean up unused imports across components
- Add prepareToolResultMessages helper for server-side message reconstruction
- Add implementation plan docs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: skip Related questions when response ends with pending interactive tool

When the AI responds with a displayOptionList (or any client-side tool
without execute), the agent stops to wait for user input. Related
questions should not appear in this state since the user hasn't answered
the clarifying question yet. This adds a guard that detects unresolved
tool calls in the response messages and skips streaming related questions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* style: fix prettier formatting for CI

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add pre-commit hook with husky + lint-staged

Runs prettier and eslint --fix on staged .ts/.tsx files, and prettier
on other file types. Prevents formatting CI failures at commit time.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: audit fixes for interactive research intake feature

Cherry-picked from claude/code-review-audit-c77zF (13ef7b3)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: resolve type error and optimize guest guard placement from audit

- Add type assertion on .map() result to fix TS error caused by removing
  any casts (TS can't narrow through .map() over a discriminated union)
- Move guest tool-result guard earlier in route handler to fail fast
  before unnecessary cookie parsing and model selection

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: make data-table schemas permissive for LLM-generated payloads

Relax validation at tool ingestion and rendering layers so AI-produced
table payloads degrade gracefully instead of failing entirely:

- Tool schema: accept any { kind: string } format instead of strict union
- UI schema: add .passthrough() to all format/column variants
- Accept string values in statusMap and colorMap alongside objects
- Coerce unknown cell values to strings via CellValueSchema fallback
- Pass componentName to safeParse for dev-mode diagnostics

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Fix notification times displaying UTC instead of local timezone (#9278)

Match time in notifications was formatted from the raw UTC value but
labeled with the event's local timezone abbreviation. Now properly
converts UTC to local time before formatting. Also uses %-H for
single-digit hours without leading zero (e.g. "8:30" not "08:30").

Affected notifications: EventSchedule, EventLevel, MatchUpcoming.

commit.message:
fix(install): add safe-copy.js to handle broken symlinks robustly

Replace all inline `node -e` fs calls with scripts/safe-copy.js.
The helper detects and removes broken entries (symlinks, junctions,
corrupted dirs) by checking if the path is actually readable, then
uses rmSync with multiple fallbacks (rmdirSync, unlinkSync, shell rmdir).

Fixes install failure on PCs where .claude/skills is a broken symlink
that rmdirSync/unlinkSync alone cannot remove.

commit.message:
fix: skip redundant field detection for fields with sub-selections (#719)

## Summary

- Fixes false positive in `redundant_fields` lint rule where fields with
different sub-selections were incorrectly flagged as redundant
- Fields with sub-selection sets are merged during GraphQL execution, so
selecting the same field with different sub-fields is not redundant

Closes #619

## Changes

- `collect_fields_from_selection_set` now only collects leaf fields (no
sub-selection) into the fragment's field set
- Direct duplicate detection skips fields with sub-selections
- Fragment-based redundancy check skips fields with sub-selections while
still recursing into nested selection sets
- Added integration tests for both the false positive case and
confirming leaf fields are still flagged

## Test Plan

1. Run `cargo test -p graphql-linter` to verify all 153 tests pass
2. Verify the issue's example no longer produces a warning:
   ```graphql
   query NotActuallyRedundant {
     ...NotRedundantFragment
     notRedundant {
       id
     }
   }

   fragment NotRedundantFragment on Query {
     notRedundant {
       aDifferentField
     }
   }
   ```
3. Verify leaf fields are still correctly flagged:
   ```graphql
   query StillRedundant {
     ...MyFragment
     id
   }

   fragment MyFragment on Query {
     id
   }
   ```

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add full 60fps patch for v1.2: patch all 8 FPS byte access sites

The original 2-site patch only forced the main frame loop and init-time
reads to return 60fps. Ghidra analysis revealed 6 additional code paths
that read/write the FPS mode byte (struct+0x3D), including the dynamic
scene writer at 0x828DC that copies scene FPS mode from param2[0x181].

New patch sites (v1.2 offsets):
- 0x012B54, 0x012CB0: GPU command builders (force 60fps flag)
- 0x0828D8, 0x0828DC: NOP scene FPS mode load+store (root cause of
  particle effects and giant battles locking to 30fps)
- 0x10F2C4: Rendering FPS check (force 60fps path)
- 0x26C114: GPU command builder 3 (force 60fps flag)

A/B verified: baseline 29.7fps -> patched 59.7fps

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix researcher: implement agentic loop for web_search multi-turn responses

The web_search tool causes stop_reason='tool_use' on the first API call,
meaning the JSON text block never appears in the initial response. The previous
code fell through to the name-only fallback every time, so no personalization
was ever injected into the PDFs.

Now implements a proper agentic loop: appends assistant turns and tool_result
messages, re-calls the API until stop_reason='end_turn', then parses the
final text block as JSON.

commit.message:
fix: Persistent sidebar — remove useLocation from App/layouts to stop full-page re-renders

Root cause: useLocation() in App.tsx caused the entire component tree to
re-render on every navigation, creating new route element references that
broke React Router v7's internal startTransition. This made the Suspense
fallback flash on every page change, giving a full-page refresh feel.

Fix:
- Extract route guards (AuthRedirect, PortalGuard, MobilePortalGuard)
  so App never subscribes to location changes
- Move useIdlePrefetch into an isolated IdlePrefetcher component
- Sidebar reads useLocation() internally for active highlighting
- Revert PrefetchLink back to <Link> (the <a>+startTransition was a no-op)
- Remove useLocation from AdminLayout (same fix)

Now on navigation: App doesn't re-render → route elements keep stable
refs → React Router uses startTransition → old page stays visible while
lazy chunks load → sidebar never flickers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix Whop: switch from /v2/checkout_configurations to /api/v2/checkout_sessions

The v2/checkout_configurations endpoint requires special permissions the API key
doesn't have. The /api/v2/checkout_sessions endpoint works with standard API keys.

Flow: for preset amounts (200/500/1000 MAD) use pre-created plan_id; for custom
amounts create a plan first via /api/v2/plans, then create checkout session.

commit.message:
fix(constellation): visible colors, slow tunnel scroll, flipped timeline, backbone markers

- Switch NodeCloud from meshStandardMaterial to meshBasicMaterial — renders
  instance colors directly without lighting dependency (was all white due to
  ambient 0.15 + emissive wash). Brightness range 0.5-1.3.
- Tunnel scroll speed reduced 7x (impulse 0.012, max velocity 3) for smooth
  gentle wormhole glide instead of instant end-to-end jumps
- Left-click drag now rotates camera in tunnel, right-click drag zooms (dolly),
  with polar angle limits to prevent flipping

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add flexible installation for macOS and GPU platforms

Solves the version compatibility problem between macOS (needs PyTorch 2.0.1)
and GPU platforms (can use PyTorch 2.4+).

## New Files

- **INSTALLATION.md**: Comprehensive installation guide for all platforms
  - Quick install by platform (macOS vs GPU)
  - Detailed explanation of version differences
  - Three installation methods (pip, requirements files, pyproject extras)
  - Troubleshooting and verification steps

- **requirements/gpu.txt**: GPU-optimized requirements with latest versions
  - PyTorch 2.4+, transformers 4.36+
  - Works on Linux/Colab/Cloud, NOT on macOS

## Changes

- **pyproject.toml**: Added [gpu] optional dependency
  - `pip install -e .` → macOS-compatible (PyTorch 2.0.1)
  - `pip install -e ".[gpu]"` → GPU-optimized (PyTorch 2.4+)
  - `pip install -e ".[all-gpu]"` → Everything with GPU versions

- **README.md**: Updated installation section
  - Shows platform-specific options
  - References INSTALLATION.md for details

## Usage

```bash
# macOS
pip install -e .

# Linux/Colab/Cloud with GPU
pip install -e ".[gpu]"
```

Users can now choose the right installation for their platform!

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Charts: Refactor Legend API — consolidate styling props and decouple from visx (#47454)

* refactor(charts): Consolidate legend styling props

The Legend component inherited 10 individual styling props from
visx's LegendOrdinal (shapeWidth, shapeHeight, shapeMargin,
labelAlign, labelFlex, labelMargin, itemMargin, itemDirection,
legendLabelProps, shapeStyle), leaking visx internals into our
public API.

Replace them with three structured style objects — itemStyles,
labelStyles, and shapeStyles — that group related concerns and
provide a cleaner, more idiomatic React API. This decouples
consumers from visx's prop surface so future dependency changes
don't break the public contract.

Updates Legend, LeaderboardChart, stories, tests, and API docs.

CHARTS-177

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Move maxWidth and textOverflow into labelStyles

These props control label text rendering and belong with other label
styling concerns in labelStyles rather than as top-level Legend props.

Chart components (BarChart, LineChart, PieChart, PieSemiCircleChart)
now forward legendMaxWidth/legendTextOverflow through labelStyles.
The chart-level API (BaseChartProps) remains unchanged.

Made-with: Cursor

* chore(charts): Add changelog for legend styling consolidation

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Address PR review feedback

Omit unused `children` prop from LegendOrdinalProps since
BaseLegend never forwards it to LegendOrdinal. Reuse the shared
LegendShapeStyles type in LeaderboardChartProps instead of
defining it inline.

Made-with: Cursor

* fix(charts): Correct Legend style type definitions

Widen LegendItemStyles.flexDirection to include 'row-reverse' and
'column-reverse' since BaseLegend uses 'row-reverse' internally
for vertical + end alignment. Without this, consumers who try to
pass those values get a TypeScript error.

Use CSSProperties['margin'] for margin types in LegendItemStyles
and LegendShapeStyles instead of manual `string | number`, for
consistency with LegendLabelStyles which already uses Pick from
CSSProperties.

Made-with: Cursor

* perf(charts): Extract duplicate items.find() in legend render

The legend value lookup was calling items.find() twice per label —
once to check existence, once to render. Extract to a variable at
the top of the map callback to avoid the redundant traversal.

Made-with: Cursor

* fix(charts): Use valid justify-content default for legend labels

'left' is not a valid CSS justify-content value for flex containers.
Replace with 'flex-start' which has the same visual effect and is
the correct flex property value.

Made-with: Cursor

* Update projects/js-packages/charts/changelog/charts-177-legend-consolidate-styling-props-into-itemstyles-and

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(charts): Narrow BaseLegendProps to used visx props only

BaseLegendProps extended the full LegendOrdinalProps surface via
Omit, leaking visx props that BaseLegend never forwards. This is
inconsistent with every other chart component which defines its
own props and selectively imports visx types. A visx update could
silently widen or break our public API.

Switch from Omit to Pick, exposing only the 6 visx props that
BaseLegend actually uses: className, shape, fill, size,
labelFormat, and labelTransform.

Also update API docs to include row-reverse and column-reverse
in LegendItemStyles.flexDirection, matching the exported type.

Made-with: Cursor

* fix(charts): Use index-based value lookup in legend render

items.find() by label text can fail when labelTransform changes
label.text, and the truthy check (itemValue &&) hides legitimate
values like 0. Use items[i]?.value for consistency with glyph
rendering, and null-check instead of truthy-check.

Made-with: Cursor

* docs(charts): Fix stale defaults in legend API and docs

The API docs still referenced justifyContent: 'left' after the
code default was corrected to 'flex-start'. The feature docs
incorrectly stated both maxWidth and textOverflow are required,
but textOverflow defaults to 'wrap' when omitted.

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Inline legend value lookup

Remove the extracted itemValue variable added in c44af61 and
inline the items.find() call at the render site. The variable
extraction was a premature optimization — the find is only
called once per label in the map callback.

Made-with: Cursor

* Revert "refactor(charts): Narrow BaseLegendProps to used visx props only"

This reverts commit 754a04759490c992396265086b1309154fdf4e48.

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Rename Legend props to drop redundant prefix

The legend prefix on legendItemClassName, legendItemProps, and
legendLabelProps is redundant within the Legend component itself —
consumers already know they're configuring a Legend. Renaming to
itemClassName, itemProps, and labelProps creates a consistent
naming pattern grouped by concern (items/labels/shapes) and
decouples labelProps from the visx legendLabelProps naming.

Chart-level props (e.g. legendItemClassName on BaseChartProps)
retain the prefix since disambiguation is needed there.

CHARTS-177

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Replace itemProps/labelProps with labelClassName

The generic pass-through props (itemProps, labelProps) formalized
an accidental pattern from the old visx rest spread — legend-level
attributes silently applied to every child element. No consumers
used this behavior, and the only real use case for legendLabelProps
was adding a class name.

Replace both with labelClassName, which mirrors itemClassName and
keeps the API minimal. Generic pass-through can be re-added if a
concrete use case surfaces.

CHARTS-177

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Narrow BaseLegendProps to used visx props

BaseLegendProps extended LegendOrdinalProps via Omit, leaking visx
props (style, data-*, event handlers) that BaseLegend never
forwards. Consumers could pass these props without TypeScript
errors but they'd be silently ignored at runtime.

Switch from Omit to Pick, exposing only the 6 visx props that
BaseLegend actually uses: className, shape, fill, size,
labelFormat, and labelTransform. Also update changelog to reflect
the full scope of API changes in this PR.

CHARTS-177

Made-with: Cursor

* test(charts): Tighten legend text wrapping assertions

The existing tests only checked that items rendered (toHaveLength)
without verifying that labelStyles were actually applied. Now each
test asserts the expected inline styles (maxWidth, minWidth) on the
label text spans, catching regressions in style forwarding.

Made-with: Cursor

* docs(charts): Align margin type in docs with source

The API docs showed margin as `string | number` but the actual
exported types use `CSSProperties['margin']`. They resolve to
the same values at runtime, but the docs should mirror the source
to avoid confusion when cross-referencing.

Made-with: Cursor

* test(charts): Assert shapeStyles margin instead of width/height

visx's LegendShape uses the `size` callback to determine width/height,
overriding explicit values. Assert on `margin` instead, which visx
always applies directly to the .visx-legend-shape element.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

visx's LegendOrdinal (shapeWidth, shapeHeight, shapeMargin,
labelAlign, labelFlex, labelMargin, itemMargin, itemDirection,
legendLabelProps, shapeStyle), leaking visx internals into our
public API.

Replace them with three structured style objects — itemStyles,
labelStyles, and shapeStyles — that group related concerns and
provide a cleaner, more idiomatic React API. This decouples
consumers from visx's prop surface so future dependency changes
don't break the public contract.

Updates Legend, LeaderboardChart, stories, tests, and API docs.

CHARTS-177

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Move maxWidth and textOverflow into labelStyles

These props control label text rendering and belong with other label
styling concerns in labelStyles rather than as top-level Legend props.

Chart components (BarChart, LineChart, PieChart, PieSemiCircleChart)
now forward legendMaxWidth/legendTextOverflow through labelStyles.
The chart-level API (BaseChartProps) remains unchanged.

Made-with: Cursor

* chore(charts): Add changelog for legend styling consolidation

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Address PR review feedback

Omit unused `children` prop from LegendOrdinalProps since
BaseLegend never forwards it to LegendOrdinal. Reuse the shared
LegendShapeStyles type in LeaderboardChartProps instead of
defining it inline.

Made-with: Cursor

* fix(charts): Correct Legend style type definitions

Widen LegendItemStyles.flexDirection to include 'row-reverse' and
'column-reverse' since BaseLegend uses 'row-reverse' internally
for vertical + end alignment. Without this, consumers who try to
pass those values get a TypeScript error.

Use CSSProperties['margin'] for margin types in LegendItemStyles
and LegendShapeStyles instead of manual `string | number`, for
consistency with LegendLabelStyles which already uses Pick from
CSSProperties.

Made-with: Cursor

* perf(charts): Extract duplicate items.find() in legend render

The legend value lookup was calling items.find() twice per label —
once to check existence, once to render. Extract to a variable at
the top of the map callback to avoid the redundant traversal.

Made-with: Cursor

* fix(charts): Use valid justify-content default for legend labels

'left' is not a valid CSS justify-content value for flex containers.
Replace with 'flex-start' which has the same visual effect and is
the correct flex property value.

Made-with: Cursor

* Update projects/js-packages/charts/changelog/charts-177-legend-consolidate-styling-props-into-itemstyles-and

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor(charts): Narrow BaseLegendProps to used visx props only

BaseLegendProps extended the full LegendOrdinalProps surface via
Omit, leaking visx props that BaseLegend never forwards. This is
inconsistent with every other chart component which defines its
own props and selectively imports visx types. A visx update could
silently widen or break our public API.

Switch from Omit to Pick, exposing only the 6 visx props that
BaseLegend actually uses: className, shape, fill, size,
labelFormat, and labelTransform.

Also update API docs to include row-reverse and column-reverse
in LegendItemStyles.flexDirection, matching the exported type.

Made-with: Cursor

* fix(charts): Use index-based value lookup in legend render

items.find() by label text can fail when labelTransform changes
label.text, and the truthy check (itemValue &&) hides legitimate
values like 0. Use items[i]?.value for consistency with glyph
rendering, and null-check instead of truthy-check.

Made-with: Cursor

* docs(charts): Fix stale defaults in legend API and docs

The API docs still referenced justifyContent: 'left' after the
code default was corrected to 'flex-start'. The feature docs
incorrectly stated both maxWidth and textOverflow are required,
but textOverflow defaults to 'wrap' when omitted.

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Inline legend value lookup

Remove the extracted itemValue variable added in c44af61 and
inline the items.find() call at the render site. The variable
extraction was a premature optimization — the find is only
called once per label in the map callback.

Made-with: Cursor

* Revert "refactor(charts): Narrow BaseLegendProps to used visx props only"

This reverts commit 754a04759490c992396265086b1309154fdf4e48.

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Rename Legend props to drop redundant prefix

The legend prefix on legendItemClassName, legendItemProps, and
legendLabelProps is redundant within the Legend component itself —
consumers already know they're configuring a Legend. Renaming to
itemClassName, itemProps, and labelProps creates a consistent
naming pattern grouped by concern (items/labels/shapes) and
decouples labelProps from the visx legendLabelProps naming.

Chart-level props (e.g. legendItemClassName on BaseChartProps)
retain the prefix since disambiguation is needed there.

CHARTS-177

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Replace itemProps/labelProps with labelClassName

The generic pass-through props (itemProps, labelProps) formalized
an accidental pattern from the old visx rest spread — legend-level
attributes silently applied to every child element. No consumers
used this behavior, and the only real use case for legendLabelProps
was adding a class name.

Replace both with labelClassName, which mirrors itemClassName and
keeps the API minimal. Generic pass-through can be re-added if a
concrete use case surfaces.

CHARTS-177

Made-with: Cursor

* refactor(charts): Narrow BaseLegendProps to used visx props

BaseLegendProps extended LegendOrdinalProps via Omit, leaking visx
props (style, data-*, event handlers) that BaseLegend never
forwards. Consumers could pass these props without TypeScript
errors but they'd be silently ignored at runtime.

Switch from Omit to Pick, exposing only the 6 visx props that
BaseLegend actually uses: className, shape, fill, size,
labelFormat, and labelTransform. Also update changelog to reflect
the full scope of API changes in this PR.

CHARTS-177

Made-with: Cursor

* test(charts): Tighten legend text wrapping assertions

The existing tests only checked that items rendered (toHaveLength)
without verifying that labelStyles were actually applied. Now each
test asserts the expected inline styles (maxWidth, minWidth) on the
label text spans, catching regressions in style forwarding.

Made-with: Cursor

* docs(charts): Align margin type in docs with source

The API docs showed margin as `string | number` but the actual
exported types use `CSSProperties['margin']`. They resolve to
the same values at runtime, but the docs should mirror the source
to avoid confusion when cross-referencing.

Made-with: Cursor

* test(charts): Assert shapeStyles margin instead of width/height

visx's LegendShape uses the `size` callback to determine width/height,
overriding explicit values. Assert on `margin` instead, which visx
always applies directly to the .visx-legend-shape element.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix walk time: use Promise-based DistanceMatrix API

Newer Google Maps JS API returns a Promise from getDistanceMatrix
instead of using callback. Switch to await-based call.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add stock photos, branded C favicon, and CalTech Web footer credit

- Download 4 Unsplash stock photos (graduation, student studying, entrepreneur, campus)
- Homepage: graduation photo in About section, student studying banner above Apply form
- About page: entrepreneur image in Professional Background section
- Contact page: campus banner image above contact grid
- Replace favicon.ico with SVG "C" favicon matching site teal color
- Add "Built by CalTech Web" credit in footer linking to caltechweb.com

Add admin reschedule feature, status filter, and mandatory staff reschedule reason

- Admin can now reschedule appointments from both calendar and list views
- Added status filter pills (Scheduled, Confirmed, Rescheduled, etc.)
- Reschedule reasons displayed on rescheduled appointments
- Staff reschedule reason changed from optional to required
